2605.12262.title: Missingness-MDPs: Bridging the Theory of Missing Data and POMDPs
2605.12262.paper_summary: We introduce missingness-MDPs (miss-MDPs), a novel subclass of partially observable Markov decision processes (POMDPs) that incorporates the theory of missing data. A miss-MDP is a POMDP whose observation function is a missingness function, specifying the probability that individual state features are missing (i.e., unobserved) at a time step. The literature distinguishes three canonical missingness types: missing (1) completely at random (MCAR), (2) at random (MAR), and (3) not at random (MNAR). Our planning problem is to compute near-optimal policies for a miss-MDP with an unknown missingness function, given a dataset of action-observation trajectories. Achieving such optimality guarantees for policies requires learning the missingness function from data, which is infeasible for general POMDPs. To overcome this challenge, we exploit the structural properties of different missingness types to derive probably approximately correct (PAC) algorithms for learning the missingness function. These algorithms yield an approximate but fully specified miss-MDP that we solve using off-the-shelf planning methods. We prove that, with high probability, the resulting policies are epsilon-optimal in the true miss-MDP. Empirical results confirm the theory and demonstrate superior performance of our approach over two model-free POMDP methods.
2605.12262.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹缺失性馬可夫決策過程（miss-MDPs），這是一種新穎的部分可觀察馬可夫決策過程（POMDPs）子類，融合了缺失數據的理論。 miss-MDP 是一個 POMDP，其觀察函數是一個缺失性函數，指定在某個時間步驟中個別狀態特徵缺失（即未觀察到）的概率。文獻中區分了三種典型的缺失性類型：缺失（1）完全隨機（MCAR）、（2）隨機（MAR）和（3）非隨機（MNAR）。我們的規劃問題是計算具有未知缺失性函數的 miss-MDP 的近似最佳策略，前提是給定一組行動-觀察軌跡數據集。為政策實現這樣的最佳性保證需要從數據中學習缺失性函數，這對於一般的 POMDP 是不可行的。為了克服這一挑戰，我們利用不同缺失性類型的結構特性來推導出學習缺失性函數的概率近似正確（PAC）算法。這些算法產生了一個近似但完全指定的 miss-MDP，我們使用現成的規劃方法來解決。 我們證明，在高概率下，所得到的策略在真實的 miss-MDP 中是 epsilon-optimal。實證結果確認了理論並展示了我們的方法在兩種無模型 POMDP 方法上的優越性能。
2605.12262.author: Joshua Wendland et.al.
2605.12262.authors: Joshua Wendland, Markel Zubia, Roman Andriushchenko, Maris F. L. Galesloot, Milan Ceska, Henrik von Kleist, Thiago D. Simao, Maximilian Weininger, Nils Jansen
